name: Jayatirtha
dates_notes: Pontifical succession records; dated by manuscript and citation.
core_thesis:
Jayatīrtha — *Ṭīkācārya*, "Master of Sub-Commentary" — is the systematiser through whom Madhva's *Tattvavāda* becomes a fully scholastic *darśana*. He introduces no new doctrine. What he supplies is dialectical apparatus: each Madhva position is restated in formal *pramāṇa*-and-*anumāna* form, each contested *adhikaraṇa* of the *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* and each contested *kārikā* of the *Anuvyākhyāna* is fixed against alternative construals, and the school's standard answers to its principal opponents are engineered.

Four interpretive choices govern the corpus. First, *bheda* (difference) is *svarūpa-svabhāva* — constitutive of the natures of the relata — and not an additional entity attached to those natures. The standard regress-objection ("if *A* differs from *B*, a further *bheda* is needed to differentiate *A*'s *bheda* from *B*'s *bheda*, and so on") fails because it presupposes that *bheda* is the kind of item that needs further differentiation; on the *svarūpa-svabhāva* construal it is not.

Second, *viśeṣa* is a self-distinguishing relational element that grounds intra-substantial differentiation without numerical multiplication of substances. Where Madhva had introduced the device, Jayatīrtha specifies the conditions under which it can be appealed to and the inferences in which it can do work.

Third, *sākṣi* (witness-consciousness) is a *pramāṇa* in its own right; its verdicts are not subject to *bādha* (sublation) by mediated cognition. The asymmetry runs in one direction only: inference may be corrected by *sākṣi*, but not the reverse.

Fourth, the *jīva*'s relation to Viṣṇu as *bimba-pratibimba* (original-and-image) is asymmetric. The image owes everything to the original; the original owes nothing to the image. Reciprocity here is excluded.

Vyāsatīrtha's *Nyāyāmṛta* and Rāghavendra's *Pariṃala* both presuppose this scaffolding.

engaged_works.title: Nyaya-Sudha
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Madhva's Anuvyākhyāna.
engaged_works.summary:
Jayatīrtha's long prose commentary on Madhva's *Anuvyākhyāna*. Together the two texts constitute the spinal scholastic apparatus of the school; for the later tradition, the *Sudhā* rather than the *Anuvyākhyāna* itself is the base-text of technical exegesis.

The method is verse by verse across all four *adhyāyas*. For each *kārikā*, Jayatīrtha reconstructs Madhva's compressed argument as a formal *anumāna*: *pakṣa* (subject), *sādhya* (predicate-to-be-established), *hetu* (reason), *vyāpti* (universal concomitance), *udāharaṇa* (illustration), and *upanaya–nigamana* (application and conclusion). Each *pūrvapakṣa* — Advaitic, Bhāskarīya, Naiyāyika, Buddhist, Mīmāṃsaka — is identified and answered in turn. Where the *Bhāṣya* admits more than one construal, the *Sudhā* fixes the school's reading.

Four technical achievements are concentrated here. *Bheda* is established as *svarūpa-svabhāva*, blocking the standard regress-objection at the level of the *vyāpti*-statement. *Viśeṣa* is given conditions of application — when it may legitimately be invoked, what it does and does not commit one to. *Sākṣi* is established as an independent *pramāṇa* by an argument from the irreducibility of first-personal givenness to inferential reconstruction. The *bimba-pratibimba* relation between Viṣṇu and *jīva* is shown to require strict asymmetry: any reciprocal construal collapses into one of the positions Madhva's *Upādhi-Khaṇḍana* has already refuted.

Vyāsatīrtha builds the *Nyāyāmṛta* on this base; Rāghavendra builds the *Pariṃala* on it. The traditional saying *yadi sudhā na pīyeta, kaḥ sa-jīvet* — "if one has not drunk the *Sudhā*, who can be said to live?" — is internal-school index of its standing.

engaged_works.title: Tattva-Prakashika on BSB
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Madhva's Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya.
engaged_works.summary: Jayatīrtha's *ṭīkā* directly on Madhva's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya*, where the *Nyāya-Sudhā* glosses the *Anuvyākhyāna*. Shorter and more closely word-locked than the *Sudhā*, the *Tattva-Prakāśikā* ("Illuminator of the Real") is the principal Mādhva *vyākhyāna* on the *Sūtra*-text itself, supplying the school's standard division into *adhikaraṇa*-units, the *viṣaya*–*saṃśaya*–*pūrvapakṣa*–*siddhānta*–*saṅgati* schema for each, and the philological resolution of contested *padacchedas*. It is on the *Tattva-Prakāśikā* in turn that Vyāsatīrtha builds the *Tātparya-Candrikā* (a sub-sub-commentary), and on the *Candrikā* that Rāghavendra builds further *Prakāśa* sub-commentaries — the layered structure that defines the school's textual life. Doctrinally the work is the locus where Jayatīrtha most directly answers Śaṅkara's reading of the *Sūtras* line by line, providing the Mādhva counter-construal of *athāto brahma-jijñāsā*, the *adhyāsa-bhāṣya*, the *ānanda-mayādhikaraṇa*, and the *ghaṭākāśa* analogy at *Brahma-Sūtra* 2.3.43.

engaged_works.title: Vadavali
engaged_works.summary: An independent polemical *prakaraṇa* in which Jayatīrtha argues for the reality of *bheda* directly, outside the commentarial frame. The text proceeds by considering the principal positive theories on which *bheda* might be "reduced" — Advaitic *vivarta* with *bheda* as superimposed, Bhāskara-style *aupādhika-bheda*, Nyāya-style *bheda* as a separate *padārtha* — and shows in each case that the reduction either fails on its own terms or smuggles back in the very *svarūpa-bheda* it sought to eliminate. Jayatīrtha's central positive thesis is that *bheda* is *svarūpa-svabhāva*: difference is not an additional ontological item attached to a substance but is constitutive of what the substance *is*; hence the standard regress-arguments against *bheda* (that it would itself need a further *bheda*, *ad infinitum*) misconstrue the relation. The *Vādāvalī* is short, sharp, and is the immediate ancestor of Vyāsatīrtha's *Nyāyāmṛta*-treatment of *bheda*; it is also the most accessible single-volume entry to Jayatīrtha's mature dialectical style.
